description: Residual sweetness in the finished beer, from bone-dry to dessert-like.
Perceived hop bitterness in the finish, independent of hop aroma.
Weight and fullness on the palate, from light and crisp to thick and chewy.
Smoked-malt and dark-roast character: coffee, char, campfire.
Fruit character from yeast esters, hops, or added fruit.
Acidity and sourness, from none to puckering.
Intensity of hop flavor and aroma — pine, citrus, tropical, floral — distinct from bitterness.
Bread, caramel, toffee, and toast depth from the grain bill.
Perceived sweetness: caramel, honey, vanilla, brown sugar.
Drying, astringent barrel tannin and bitter grip in the finish.
Mouthfeel weight, from light and delicate to thick and oily.
Peat smoke, barrel char, and ash character.
Orchard, citrus, and dried-fruit notes: apple, cherry, raisin, fig.
Lift and zing on the palate — citrusy, vibrant vs. flat and heavy.
Rye pepper and baking spice: cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, black pepper.
Barrel-derived flavor: vanilla, toast, coconut, toasted wood.
Residual sugar, from bone-dry to lusciously sweet.
Drying grip from grape skins and oak, mostly in reds.
Weight on the palate, from light and airy to full and dense.
Smoky, toasty character from oak or a smoky minerality.
Fruit intensity and ripeness, from subtle to jammy.
Freshness and zip; makes the mouth water.
Stony, chalky, saline notes beyond fruit and oak.
Barrel influence: vanilla, baking spice, butter, toast.
